mechanism: Cationic CI reacts with anionic SI forming insoluble salts; loss of both treatments and possible plugging
mitigation: Inject at separate points or use compatible non-ionic SI; bench test before deployment

mechanism: Cationic quaternary precipitates anionic polymer SI
mitigation: Use separate injection points or anionic CI

mechanism: Strong cationic CI can disrupt demulsifier action and stabilise emulsions
mitigation: Bench test compatibility; consider lower CI dose

mechanism: Most film-forming CIs require pH > 3 to remain in solution; degrade in strong acid
mitigation: Use acid-resistant CI (acid corrosion inhibitor) during stimulation

mechanism: Phosphonate hydrolyses in concentrated HCl above 60 C; loss of inhibition
mitigation: Squeeze SI before acid stimulation; use acid-stable polymeric SI

mechanism: Phosphonate forms Ca-phosphonate precipitate above critical Ca concentration
mitigation: Switch to polyacrylate or polymaleate SI

mechanism: Iron interferes with phosphonate and polymer SI; can form Fe-SI complexes
mitigation: Add iron control (chelant or reducing agent) upstream of SI

mechanism: Triazine decomposes above 80 C releasing amines and reducing scavenging capacity
mitigation: Use iron-based scavenger or inject downstream of cooling

mechanism: High pH triazine raises local pH causing CaCO3 precipitation
mitigation: Inject scavenger and SI at separate points

mechanism: Spent triazine forms dithiazine which can deposit; high Ca aggravates
mitigation: Monitor for dithiazine deposits; reduce overdose

mechanism: Oxidising biocide consumed by reducing scavenger; both treatments lost
mitigation: Use non-oxidising biocide (glutaraldehyde) or separate injection

mechanism: Glutaraldehyde reacts with primary amines in triazine
mitigation: Stagger injection in time

mechanism: Some SIs stabilise water-in-oil emulsions and antagonise demulsifiers
mitigation: Confirm with bottle test

mechanism: Solvent carrier may dissolve antagonistic to other
mitigation: Confirm solvent compatibility

mechanism: High MEG concentration reduces water activity and accelerates Ca-phosphonate precipitation
mitigation: Use polymeric SI in MEG service

mechanism: Methanol can wash off CI film; may need higher dose
mitigation: Increase CI dose by 25-50% or use stronger filming agent

mechanism: Acid will react with carbonate forming CO2; expected reaction during stimulation
mitigation: Design acid volume for stoichiometry; monitor effluent pH

mechanism: HCl above 5wt% causes severe corrosion of carbon steel above 60 C without inhibitor
mitigation: Always co-inject acid corrosion inhibitor (propargyl alcohol-based)

mechanism: HF causes severe corrosion and forms iron fluoride scale
mitigation: Use mud acid only with proper inhibitor; consider CRA tubulars

mechanism: EDTA can sequester Ca and reduce phosphonate-Ca synergy
mitigation: Use sub-stoichiometric chelant

mechanism: Caustic raises pH causing CaCO3 and Mg(OH)2 scale
mitigation: Avoid caustic in calcium-rich water; use buffer instead

mechanism: Low pH protonates phosphonate reducing Ca complexation efficiency
mitigation: Maintain pH 4-6 for phosphonate

mechanism: Silicone antifoam can stabilise oil-in-water emulsions and foul demulsifier
mitigation: Use polyglycol antifoam or minimise dose

mechanism: Drag reducer can interfere with separator performance
mitigation: Inject downstream of separation

mechanism: Methanol can salt out KHI polymer at high concentration
mitigation: Limit MeOH below 10 wt% in KHI service

mechanism: AA chemistry stabilises emulsions; antagonises demulsifier
mitigation: Increase demulsifier dose; bottle test

mechanism: Most film-forming CIs degrade above 150 C losing protection
mitigation: Use high temperature CI (phosphate ester) or CRA upgrade

mechanism: Phosphonate SI thermally degrades above 175 C
mitigation: Use polymeric SI or alternative chemistry

mechanism: Iron from scavenger interferes with phosphonate SI
mitigation: Stagger injection or use chelant pretreatment

mechanism: Bisulphite catalysed decomposition above 80 C
mitigation: Use catalysed bisulphite for higher temperature

mechanism: THPS can react with amines reducing biocide and scavenger
mitigation: Stagger or use glutaraldehyde

chemical1Type: # Phase 6 additions: NACE / NORSOK / API / ASTM provenance rules

mechanism: [NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156] Sour service threshold pH2S>0.0034 bar requires SSC-resistant CRA or qualified inhibitor
mitigation: Verify material per ISO 15156-2/3; ensure CI qualified for sour service

mechanism: [NORSOK M-506] CO2 corrosion model assumes flow-induced film removal above 5 m/s in carbon steel
mitigation: Apply Tulsa flow correction; consider CRA or duplex

mechanism: [NACE TM0374] High ionic strength reduces phosphonate effectiveness via ion shielding
mitigation: Increase dose by 30-50% or use sulphonated polymer

mechanism: [NORSOK M-001] Quaternary CI may not be required for 316L below 60 C but film aids erosion-corrosion
mitigation: Confirm with field exposure coupons per NACE SP0775

mechanism: [GPSA Engineering Data Book Sec 22] Triazine derivatives mildly corrosive at low T; coupon-monitor per NACE SP0775
mitigation: Use SS316 contactor or batch dose

mechanism: [NACE TM0169] Below 10 ppb O2 the corrosion contribution is negligible; scavenger overdose is wasteful
mitigation: Tune scavenger dose to maintain 5-10 ppb residual O2

mechanism: [NACE TM0212] Glutaraldehyde efficacy reduced by H2S consumption; SRB control needs alternation
mitigation: Alternate with THPS or quaternary biocide

mechanism: [NACE MR0175] Phosphate ester thermally stable to ~200 C but pitting risk on 316L in chloride above 200 C
mitigation: Switch to duplex 2205/2507 or batch CI

mechanism: [NACE TM0374] BaSO4 scale extremely insoluble; standard SIs need 2-5x normal dose
mitigation: Use phosphino-polymer at MIC; squeeze treatment recommended

mechanism: [NACE TM0193] EDTA above 80 C complexes Fe and accelerates carbon-steel general corrosion
mitigation: Use HEDTA or CRA for hot service

mechanism: [NACE MR0175] HCl above 5 wt% causes pitting and crevice corrosion of 316L above 60 C
mitigation: Use Hastelloy C-276 or batch acid with corrosion inhibitor

mechanism: [NACE SP0775] Organic acids form Fe-acetate complexes accelerating top-of-line corrosion
mitigation: Verify CI compatibility with organic acids; use acid-CI

mechanism: [NORSOK M-506] Below 5% water cut continuous CI inefficient (poor water wetting)
mitigation: Use batch CI or dispersant package; monitor with FSM probe

mechanism: [ASTM D97 / D5853] EVA effectiveness drops sharply below cloud point; pour-point depression limited
mitigation: Use poly-acrylate or olefin-ester WPPD; or consider thermal management

mechanism: [Yen-Mullins / NACE TM0102] CO2 destabilises asphaltenes by reducing solvent power
mitigation: Increase dispersant dose 50-100% or treat upstream of CO2 injection

mechanism: [NACE TM0194] THPS chelates Fe3+ producing scale-inhibition synergy but consumes biocide
mitigation: Tune dose with on-site SRB MPN testing

mechanism: [NACE TM0374] Caustic raises pH above 9 nucleating CaCO3; SI overwhelmed
mitigation: Switch to amine buffer or stage neutralisation

mechanism: [NACE SP0775] Cationic CI can collapse anionic DRA polymer at injection point
mitigation: Inject DRA downstream of CI or use compatible non-ionic DRA

mechanism: [NACE TM0169] Catalysed bisulphite decomposes above 120 C releasing SO2 (corrosive)
mitigation: Use erythorbate or vacuum deaeration
